question_text: Which of these is an example of balanced forces?

options.label: A
options.text: A book sliding across a table and slowing down

options.label: B
options.text: A car accelerating from a traffic light

options.label: C
options.text: A book sitting still on a table

options.label: D
options.text: A ball being kicked across grass

correct_answer: A book sitting still on a table
explanation: When a book sits still on a table, the downward force of gravity (weight) is balanced by the upward normal force from the table. Since the forces are balanced, there is no net force, so the book remains at rest. In contrast, options A, B, and D all involve objects that are either slowing down or accelerating, which means unbalanced forces are acting on them.
